# R0.2 · Valkey under codemojex

> Where Valkey sits in codemojex: the platform reaches one boundary, the echo data layer; below it
> Valkey serves two roles — EchoStore, the near-cache (L1 ETS over L2 Valkey), and the EchoMQ bus, the
> owned-protocol bus at echo/apps/echo_mq — both over one owned client, EchoWire. No surface holds a raw Valkey
> key. Grounded in the as-built code (echo/apps/echo_wire, echo_mq, echo_store) and the committed BCS record
> (docs/echo/bcs/content/bcs3.*, bcs4.*), backed by Valkey. A teaching module: a hub and three dives. Served at
> /redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game): the placement — the facade seam, the two roles below it, and the tier retold.
- [The facade seam](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/the-facade-seam): the master invariant — every surface calls only the echo data layer and reads a typed result from a closed set; EchoWire is the one owned Valkey client below the seam, and even the wire's boot is typed, by the version fence.
- [The two roles](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/two-roles): EchoStore, the derived near-cache (caching/modeling families — L1 ETS over L2 Valkey) vs the EchoMQ bus, the authoritative state (queues/coordination/streams/flow families — the three-field job hash, the four sorted sets).
- [The reserved tier](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game/reserved-tier): the tier once held in reserve, now shipped — EchoMQ at echo/apps/echo_mq, the BCS 2.0 Valkey-native bus and the convergence target of the EchoMQ program (the v1 line frozen at 1.3.0 is the push source); the worked move is the real claim script from bcs3.3, and the doors to /echomq and /bcs are live routes.

## Up

- [R0 · Overview](/redis-patterns/overview) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
